# preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts { #spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts }

`preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts`

Linear interpolation transformer for time series data.

## Classes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [LinearlyInterpolateTS](#spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS) | Transformer that applies linear interpolation to time series data. |

### LinearlyInterpolateTS { #spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS }

```python
preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS(on_missing='raise')
```

Transformer that applies linear interpolation to time series data.

The transformer always runs ``y.interpolate(method="linear")`` first.
The ``on_missing`` keyword then chooses how to handle any NaN that
linear interpolation cannot bridge (typically the leading and
trailing endpoints of the series).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type                                                                              | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Default   |
|------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| on_missing | [OnMissing](`spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.OnMissing`) | Contract for residual NaN after linear interpolation.  - ``"raise"`` (default, fail-safe): raise ``ValueError`` if   any NaN remains. Refuses to silently embed imputed values   disguised as measurements. - ``"ffill_bfill"``: explicit opt-in to endpoint   forward-then-backward fill, so that leading and trailing   NaNs are both bridged. - ``"passthrough"``: return the linearly interpolated   series unchanged. The caller promises to handle the   residual NaN downstream. | `'raise'` |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                       | Description                                                                                                                                           |
|--------|----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If ``on_missing`` is not one of the three accepted values, or if ``on_missing="raise"`` (the default) and any NaN remains after linear interpolation. |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> import pandas as pd
>>> import numpy as np
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts import LinearlyInterpolateTS
```

Interior gaps are bridged by linear interpolation under every
mode; the default ``"raise"`` then succeeds because nothing
remains:

```python
>>> s = pd.Series([1.0, np.nan, 3.0])
>>> LinearlyInterpolateTS().fit_transform(s).tolist()
[1.0, 2.0, 3.0]
```

Endpoint NaNs are bridged only when the caller opts in
explicitly:

```python
>>> s = pd.Series([1.0, np.nan, 3.0, np.nan])
>>> LinearlyInterpolateTS(on_missing="ffill_bfill").fit_transform(s).tolist()
[1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 3.0]
```

``"passthrough"`` lets the residual NaN survive for the caller
to handle:

```python
>>> out = LinearlyInterpolateTS(on_missing="passthrough").fit_transform(s)
>>> bool(out.isna().iloc[-1])
True
```

#### Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [apply](#spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.apply) | Apply linear interpolation and dispatch on ``on_missing``. |
| [fit](#spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.fit) | Fitted transformer (no-op). |
| [transform](#spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.transform) | Transform the input data by applying linear interpolation. |

##### apply { #spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.apply }

```python
preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.apply(y)
```

Apply linear interpolation and dispatch on ``on_missing``.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                                | Description                | Default    |
|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------|------------|
| y      | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | Input Series or DataFrame. | _required_ |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                                | Description                                                |
|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | Union[pd.Series, pd.DataFrame]: The transformed data. For  |
|        | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | ``on_missing="ffill_bfill"`` any residual endpoint NaN has |
|        | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | been forward- and back-filled; for ``"passthrough"`` it    |
|        | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | survives; for ``"raise"`` (the default) the method raises  |
|        | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | instead of returning a NaN-bearing result.                 |

###### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                       | Description                                                                                                                            |
|--------|----------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If ``self.on_missing`` is not a recognized value, or if ``self.on_missing == "raise"`` and any NaN remains after linear interpolation. |

##### fit { #spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.fit }

```python
preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.fit(X, y=None)
```

Fitted transformer (no-op).

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                | Description   | Default    |
|--------|---------------------|---------------|------------|
| X      | [Any](`typing.Any`) | Input data.   | _required_ |
| y      | [Any](`typing.Any`) | Ignored.      | `None`     |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                      | Description             |
|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| self   | [LinearlyInterpolateTS](`spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS`) | The fitted transformer. |

##### transform { #spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.transform }

```python
preprocessing.linearly_interpolate_ts.LinearlyInterpolateTS.transform(X)
```

Transform the input data by applying linear interpolation.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                                | Description                               | Default    |
|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|------------|
| X      | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | Input Series or DataFrame to interpolate. | _required_ |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                                | Description                                             |
|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | Union[pd.Series, pd.DataFrame]: Interpolated data, with |
|        | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)\] | residual NaN handled according to ``self.on_missing``.  |
